Let me give you a very, very powerful syntax here, the order in which you qualify. Number one, the first thing to do is you ask questions, right? And of course, you use your justifiers when you ask those questions. Number two, you get answers based on those questions, right? You get answers based on the questions that you ask. And step three is that you ask more questions based on those answers. You essentially exhaust lines of questioning based on the answers that you get. What you don't do is you don't get an answer saying, oh my God, I got the perfect profile. You're trying to sell them right there in the spot. And this is, again, I see salespeople doing all the time when they qualify. They try to jump the gun, they start saying how great and how wonderful their product is. And again, people simply just do not buy in that order. It's not the syntax of creating certainty, right? There's so many other variables that need to be addressed before you just jump the gun and start telling them how great your product is, okay? Syntax, again, one more time. You ask questions, right? You get answers from those questions. And of course, you make mental notes based on those answers, right? You gather intelligence. That's your gathering process. And then you ask more questions in response to their answers, essentially exhausting a particular line of questioning, digging deeper if you have to. And any time you see pain, this is your next step, you have to essentially ferret out the pain, add it there in denial, you amplify it. And essentially, that is how you qualify. You know, use your justify, you ask a question, make a mental note, don't try to solve pain, then move on to your next question, okay? And if you do that, you stand this straight line of prospecting, bam, you will find yourself not only getting the right people into your pipeline, but you'll also find that when they're in there, you're in massive rapport with them. They have huge respect for you. There's this sort of emotional bond that's developed and they really believe in their heart and their mind, which should be the truth that you actually care. You're not one of these salespeople just looking to make a quick sale, stick and move, right? You're someone that's going to be there for the long term.
